LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE. DISCUSSIONS/ARGUMENT ARE HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSIONS/ARGUMENT, THE COURT DECLINED MR. SCOTT'S REQUEST TO RESERVE JURISDICTION REGARDING ANY MOTION(S) FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE IS DISCHARGED, AND THE AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DISMISSED AS MOOT. COUNSEL FOR THE RESPONDENTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR TENTATIVE RULING FOR THE COURT'S COMPETE RULING) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORR

LAW AND MOTION 302, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES. PETITIONERS' MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS' FEES PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SEC. 1021.5 IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF 59,027.00. RESPONDENT'S MOTION TO STRIKE OR TAX COSTS IS DENIED, AND PETITIONERS ARE AWARDED $1,209 IN COSTS. PETITIONERS ARE ALSO ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF "FEES ON FEES" FOR TIME SPENT IN CONNECTION WITH THE INSTANT MOTIONS. (KETCHUM V. MOSES (2001) 24 CAL.4TH 1122, 1133.) PETITIONERS SHALL, WITHIN 10 DAYS, FILE A DECLARATION OF COUNSEL DETAILING THE TIME SPENT IN CONNECTION WITH THE FEES AND COSTS MOTIONS, COUNSEL'S REASONABLE HOURLY RATES, AND THE TOTAL AMOUNT SOUGHT. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR PETITIONER IS TO SUBMIT A SEPERATE PROPOSED ORDER AWARDING PETITIONERS FEES ON FEES, IN AN AMOUNT TO BE DETERMINED TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND DENYING MOTION TO STRIKE OR TAX COSTS IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Sep-29-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances
Paul D. Scott, Esq.; (415) 981-1212; LAW OFFICES OF PAUL D. SCOTT, P.C.; dscott@lopds.com; for Petitioners.(in-person)
Suzanne Solomon, Esq; 310.981.2000; LIEBERT CASSIDY WHITMORE; ssolomon@lcwlegal.com; for Respondents SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF EDUCATION et al via ZOOM
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Court reporter.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

PETITIONER ABRAHAM LINCOLN HIGH SCHOOL OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO TAXPAYERS ASSOCIATION, GEORGE WASHINGTON HIGH SCHOOL ALUMNI ASSCIATION, JOHN BURTON, KAREN SAKATA, LOWELL ALUMNI ASSOCIATION, BALBOA HIGH SCHOOL ALUMNI ASSOCIATION, MISSION HIGH SCHOOL ALUMNI ASSOCIATION Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees;

Petitioners' motion for award of attorneys' fees pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Sec. 1021.5 is granted in the amount of 59,027.00. Respondent's motion to strike or tax costs is denied, and Petitioners are awarded $1,209 in costs. Petitioners are also entitled to an award of "fees on fees" for time spent in connection with the instant motions. (Ketchum v. Moses (2001) 24 Cal.4th 1122, 1133.) Petitioners shall, within 10 days, file a declaration of counsel detailing the time spent in connection with the fees and costs motions, counsel's reasonable hourly rates, and the total amount sought.

(see word minutes for complete details)

Order GRANTING Motion for Attorney Fees and Denying Motion to Strike or Tax Costs is signed.

Court discusses a house keeping issue with Plaintiff's counsel Mr. Scott. Court indicates that Petitioners are entitled to fees on fees; as to reasonable attorneys fees incurred in preparing the motion for an award of attorneys fees and and for attendance at today's hearing. Mr Scott is directed by the Court to file a Declaration of Counsel within ten days, detailing in an appropriate level of detail, the amount of time devoted it those activities, applying the same hourly rates requested in connection with the merits and a seperate proposed order awarding Petitioners fees on fees, in an amount to be determined and to email the Declaration and separate proposed order to contestdept302tr@sftc.org .

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)

Order To Show Cause (Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 3/18/2021)
Off Calendar May-06-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference);
Atty. Paul D. Scott, Esq. of the Law Offices of Paul D. Scott, 415.981.1212, for Petitioners;
Atty. Lani Remick, Esq. of the Law Offices of Paul D. Scott, 415.981.1212, for Petitioners;
Atty. Suzanne Solomon, Esq. of Liebert Cassidy Whitmore, 415.981.1212, for Respondents.

Order To Show Cause.

The matter is called. DISCUSSIONS/ARGUMENT are held.

At the conclusion of discussions/argument, the Court declined Mr. Scott's request to reserve jurisdiction regarding any motion(s) for attorney's fees and adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

The order to show cause is discharged, and the amended petition for writ of mandate is dismissed as moot. By the first amended petition, petitioners seek a writ of mandate ordering respondents to repeal Resolution No. 211-12A1, relating to the renaming of San Francisco Unified School District schools, as well as related declaratory and injunctive relief, on the grounds that in enacting that Resolution at its January 26, 2021 meeting, respondent Board of Education violated the Brown Act. On April 6, 2021, after suspending the work of the panel it had previously appointed to advise it regarding the renaming process, the Board rescinded Resolution 211-12A1. No school was renamed pursuant to the Board's resolutions. As a result, the parties agree that the proceeding is moot, and must be dismissed. "A case is considered moot when 'the question addressed was at one time a live issue in the case,' but has been deprived of life 'because of events occurring after the judicial process was initiated.' . . . . The pivotal question in determining if a case is moot is therefore whether the court can grant the plaintiff any effectual relief." (Wilson & Wilson v. City Council of Redwood City (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 1559, 1574 (citations omitted).) "When events render a case moot the court, whether trial or appellate, should generally dismiss it." (Id.; see, e.g., TransparentGov Novato v. City of Novato (2019) 34 Cal.App.5th 140, 150-153 [affirming dismissal of petition for violations of Brown Act, where city council adopted new policy following claimed violations, and petitioner therefore did not demonstrate a justifiable controversy warranting mandamus or declaratory relief].) The court declines petitioners' request in their late-filed reply to make findings that the action is not frivolous or to address any of the other arguments raised by the parties. "The rendering of advisory opinions falls within neither the functions nor the jurisdiction" of California courts. (People ex rel. Lynch v. Superior Court (1970) 1 Cal.3d 910, 912.).

Counsel for the respondents is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
